While Travis is busy securing the Chiefs' future, he and Taylor are reportedly deep in the "presidential-level" secrecy of planning their upcoming wedding.The couple, who got engaged in August 2025 in a flower-filled proposal in Travis’s own backyard, are reportedly eyeing June 13, 2026, for their big day. The date is a classic Taylor "Easter Egg," centering on her lucky number 13. Rumors suggest the ceremony will take place at her breathtaking Ocean House estate in Rhode Island, providing a private coastal sanctuary for what many are calling the "wedding of the century. "As Travis prepares for his 14th NFL season, it's clear his greatest motivation isn't just another trophy, it's the woman in the photo in the hallway. From a chance encounter at Arrowhead to the final countdown to their wedding day, Travis and Taylor are proving that the best stories are the ones you never see coming.
Article Summary: Travis Kelce and Taylor Swift's "Hall of Fame" moment went viral this week. Kelce gave a playful wave to a framed photo of Swift at Arrowhead Stadium. The gesture was a silent acknowledgment of the place where it all began.

Article Title: Connor Mcdavid: Connor McDavid shares how he is managing health issues to avoid joining the Edmonton Oilers injury list
Article Text:
Connor McDavid has been dealing with hip and groin issues and is managing his workload carefully to avoid any further injuries (Image via Getty Images)

Connor McDavid taking no chances with his health as the Oilers try to avoid another injury blow





As the NHL playoffs are around the corner, the Edmonton Oilers have entered a critical phase where they have to remain cautious about their players so they don't get affected by any further injuries. Owing to this situation, Oilers star Connor McDavid is reportedly taking a cautious approach with his recent physical issues with the team to navigate this critical stretch of the 2025-26 NHL season.According to recent reports, the Oilers captain has been dealing with hip and groin issues and is managing his workload carefully to avoid aggravating the injury and missing significant time. McDavid's cautious approach comes at a difficult moment for the Edmonton Oilers, especially after his fellow team member Leon Draisaitl suffered an unexpected lower-body injury, keeping him away from the club for the remainder of the season.His absence has already put pressure on the team, making McDavid's health even more important as the playoffs approach. Edmonton Oilers star Connor McDavid is busy managing his health, as the team has entered a crucial stretch of the season, and they cannot afford to miss another star player to injury in their lineup.According to reports, McDavid left a recent practice early, which raised serious concerns. However, the forward later addressed it with a simple update about his condition.He stated, “Just hips & groin stuff. Yup, all good.” Though the issue is not serious, the team remains cautious, trying to avoid another injury blow to their lineup.Meanwhile, taking cognizance of the situation, head coach Kris Knoblauch explained the decision around McDavid’s health situation, “He was on the line of taking a maintenance day. Just wanted to touch the ice (today),” the coach stated. This confirmed that the Oilers are exercising a very cautious approach, especially with the season nearing its final stage.The Oilers are anyway dealing with a major blow ahead of the NHL playoffs, as star forward Leon Draisaitl will remain absent for the remainder of the 2025-26 regular season due to his serious lower-body injury. The player sustained the injury during Edmonton’s 3–1 victory over the Nashville Predators.As for the current NHL playoff scenario, the Edmonton Oilers sit in third place in the Pacific Division of the Western Conference, with 34 wins and 28 losses in 71 games played, trailing the Anaheim Ducks and the Vegas Golden Knights, who are currently leading the points table.The Oilers will be next seen in action against the Utah Mammoth in a tightly contested playoff race, with McDavid expected to look after the offensive lineup of the team.
Article Summary: Connor McDavid has been dealing with hip and groin issues and is managing his workload carefully to avoid aggravating the injury and missing significant time. McDavid's cautious approach comes at a difficult moment for the Edmonton Oilers, especially after his fellow team member Leon Draisaitl suffered an unexpected lower-body injury.

Article Title: Joe Burrow Relationship: Fans think Joe Burrow is embarrassed of Olivia Ponton—Viral videos spark massive debate
Article Text:
(Image via Getty Images)

Is Joe Burrow protecting his image or hiding his relationship with Olivia Ponton?





In the world of the NFL, few figures command as much "cool" as Joe Burrow. The Cincinnati Bengals quarterback has built a brand on being the unflappable, cigar-smoking leader who rarely lets the public see behind the curtain. However, his high-profile relationship with TikTok star Olivia Ponton has sparked a digital firestorm, leaving fans wondering: is Joe protecting his privacy, or is he actually embarrassed of his relationship?The narrative reached a fever pitch this March following a series of sightings in Los Angeles. In several viral clips, most notably outside the Fanatics Flag Football Classic, Burrow is seen walking briskly, often several paces ahead of Ponton.To the "body language experts" of social media, this gap isn't just physical, it's emotional. Fans have been quick to label the dynamic as "cringe," pointing to the stark contrast between Joe’s stoic demeanor and Olivia’s high-energy, camera-ready presence.The speculation intensified after the Vanity Fair Oscars party, where Joe was spotted leaving with a group of A-listers including Alix Earle and Tate McRae.When Olivia wasn't immediately visible in the frame, the "breakup" hashtags began trending instantly.The "embarrassment" theory usually stems from a clash of archetypes. Joe Burrow is the "old school" athlete who treats his personal life like a playbook, tightly guarded. Olivia Ponton, conversely, is a digital native who rose to fame by sharing her most vulnerable moments, including a viral video of herself lost and crying at a Paris airport.Critics argue that Joe’s "distanced" behavior is a reaction to Olivia’s "main character energy," suggesting he’s trying to detach his professional image from the influencer spotlight.However, looking closer reveals a different story. Burrow has a documented history of keeping his romantic life out of the frame. Even during his long-term relationship with Olivia Holzmacher, he rarely participated in the "Instagram Boyfriend" trope.What the internet calls "embarrassment" might simply be a strategic play. By arriving separately or maintaining distance in front of paparazzi, Joe effectively denies the tabloids the "money shot" of a high-profile couple. Despite the rumors of a rift, Ponton was spotted on the sidelines of his game just this past weekend, proving that while they may not be "Post-Hardcore" on social media, they are very much a team behind closed doors.Is he embarrassed? Likely not. He’s just playing defense against a world that wants to consume his private life as entertainment.
Article Summary: Cincinnati Bengals quarterback Joe Burrow's relationship with TikTok star Olivia Ponton has sparked a digital firestorm. The narrative reached a fever pitch this March following a series of sightings in Los Angeles. In several viral clips, Burrow is seen walking briskly, often several paces ahead of Ponton.

Article Title: Carlos Alcaraz: Carlos Alcaraz steps away for family break after Miami Open loss to Sebastian Korda ahead of clay season
Article Text:
Carlos Alcaraz (Image Via Getty)

Carlos Alcaraz explains Miami Open loss to Sebastian Korda and his plan to reset before clay season





Carlos Alcaraz had a tough night at the 2026 Miami Open, and it ended earlier than many expected. On March 22, the World No. 1 lost to Sebastian Korda in the third round. The match went 6-3, 5-7, 6-4 in Korda’s favor. It was a strong performance from the American, but for Alcaraz, it was another early exit in Miami, something fans did not see coming.After the match, Alcaraz did not hide how he felt. Instead of rushing back into training, he spoke openly about needing a small break. The Spaniard said he wants to go home, spend time with family and friends, and clear his mind before the busy clay season begins. His focus now is not just tennis, but also getting himself mentally fresh again.Speaking after the match, Carlos Alcaraz kept things honest and simple. He said, “Well, I don’t know. I don’t know. Probably I’m going to go back home, which I’m just looking forward to, stay chilling with my family, with my friends. A couple of days. I don’t know how much they, my team, are going to allow me to have rest and days off. And all of a sudden you just go back on track, go back on the court.”He made it clear that this short break matters to him. The clay season is very close, and it includes big tournaments like Monte Carlo, Barcelona, Madrid, Rome, and Roland Garros.Alcaraz wants to be ready, both in body and mind. He added, “The clay season is around the corner and I have really good tournaments that I’m excited about playing there. My mind right now is to take some days off, reset my mind, reset batteries and be ready and in a good state for the clay season. ”This is now the second straight year where Alcaraz has struggled in Miami. Last year, he lost in the second round to David Goffin, even after winning the first set. So this year’s result will feel even more disappointing for him.At the same time, Alcaraz gave full credit to Sebastian Korda for the win. He said, “Sebi was incredible today. He played a magnificent match. There were many tense moments that I wasn’t able to take advantage of, and I think he was better in those points. I’d say that was the key to the match. Congratulations to him. He deserves it.”With this result, Korda has now improved his record against Alcaraz to two wins and four losses. The American will next face Martin Landaluce in the fourth round as his Miami Open run continues.
Article Summary: Carlos Alcaraz lost 6-3, 5-7, 6-4 to Sebastian Korda in the third round of the 2026 Miami Open. The Spaniard said he wants to go home, spend time with family and friends, and clear his mind before the busy clay season begins.

Article Title: Puka Nacua: Puka Nacua’s Los Angeles Rams linked to $61 million Buffalo Bills linebacker
Article Text:
Puka Nacua’s LA Rams linked to $61 million Buffalo Bills linebacker (Image via Getty: Los Angeles Rams logo and Buffalo Bills logo)

NFL Trade Rumors : Puka Nacua’s Los Angeles connected to Buffalo LB Matt Milano

Rams strengthen with Chiefs CBs Trent McDuffie and Jaylen Watson





CBS Sports’ Zachary Pereles pairs Buffalo Bills linebacker Matt Milano with 2026 draft prospect Anthony Hill Jr. for the Los Angeles Rams. The analyst notes Los Angeles may need wide receiver help beyond Davante Adams and Puka Nacua, but a versatile, impactful linebacker could never hurt.The Rams have Nate Landman but pairing Hill with Milano could take the linebacker corps to a different level al-together. It would also relieve a lot of stress off defensive coordinator Chris Shula’s shoulders. Now, let’s take a good look at the 31-year-old Bills linebacker and how he fits into the Rams picture.Milano was drafted by the then-Tyrod Taylor-led Buffalo in 2017 in the fifth round as the 163rd overall pick.He recorded 572 total tackles [372 solo], 14.0 sacks, five forced fumbles, 11 fumble recoveries, 39 passes defended, two defensive touchdowns, and 10 interceptions in 106 games over nine seasons playing for the Bills.The veteran LB has also had seven past injuries: grade 2 thigh hamstring strain, grade 2 chest pectoral strain, unspecified grade 1 abdomen muscle sprain/pull, grade 3 knee MCL tear, grade 1 leg tibial plateau fracture, grade 3 arm bicep tear, grade 1 inguinal groin pull, and grade 1 chest pectoral pull.According to Draft Sharks, the 31-year-old linebacker has a 49 percent chance of injury in 2026 and a 3.9 percent chance of injury per game.The one-time Pro Bowler is projected to miss 1.90 games in 2026, and his durability score [ability to produce despite relatively minor injuries] is 3.02 [5 is most durable]. He currently has no injuries. But his parent franchise has released him after nine long, committed seasons. Now, several analysts are picturing him in Los Angeles.SB Nation’s Danny Stone reports Milano’s best days are behind him, but he could have some juice left in the tank, and for that matter, he could be a veteran presence to help place Los Angeles over the top.Heavy Sports’ Josh Buckhalter reports the Rams struck gold with LBNate Landman in 2025 free agency, and this year they could repeat it with Buffalo LB. He could be the push Los Angeles needs to land a Super Bowl win.Rams Wire’s Oliver G. reports Los Angeles fixed its secondary by acquiring Kansas City Chiefs cornerbacks Trent McDuffie and Jaylen Watson, and by keeping safety Kamren Curl. They also re-signed another crucial safety: Quentin Lake.This has led several analysts to shift their focus to the offensive side of the ball in draft projections for the Rams. So that means the defensive side of the ball, that is, the linebacker position, could be filled by a veteran. That’s where Milano fits the picture for Los Angeles.Anyhow, Milano will turn 32 this July. He recorded 67 tackles, 3.5 sacks, and one forced fumble in 2025. According to Spotrac’s calculated market value, the Boston College alum is projected to sign a one-year, $4.59M contract, giving him a $4.6M average annual income. His total career earnings over nine years are $61M.Heavy Sports’ Josh Buckhalter reports the Rams have the cap space, and they are known for bargain hunting at off-ball linebacker. Something that led them to Landman. And something that could lead them to Milano. Would Puka Nacua’s Rams sign him? Only time will tell…
Article Summary: Buffalo Bills linebacker Matt Milano has been linked to the Los Angeles Rams. The 31-year-old is projected to sign a one-year, $4.59M contract. Milano recorded 572 total tackles, 14.0 sacks, five forced fumbles, 11 fumble recoveries, 39 passes defended, two defensive touchdowns, and 10 interceptions in 106 games over nine seasons.

Article Title: Bo Bichette: Bo Bichette steps into big Mets role as team moves on from Pete Alonso with new plan
Article Text:
Bo Bichette (Image Via Getty)

Marc Luino explains why Bo Bichette could fit better than Pete Alonso alongside Juan Soto and Francisco Lindor





The New York Mets are heading into the 2026 season with a very different team. After falling short in recent playoff runs, they made big changes. Pete Alonso and Edwin Diaz are no longer part of the roster. In their place, the team has brought in names like Bo Bichette, Juan Soto, Francisco Lindor, Luis Robert Jr., Marcus Semien, and Freddy Peralta. It is a fresh start, and a lot of attention is now on how this new lineup will perform.One big talking point is Bo Bichette taking over the cleanup role that Pete Alonso once held. On paper, Bichette is not known for the same home run power. But some believe he could still make a bigger impact in a different way.That idea was explained in detail by Mets analyst Marc Luino, who shared his thoughts on the Mets’d Up podcast.Speaking on the Mets’d Up podcast, Marc Luino pointed out that Bo Bichette could benefit a lot from hitting behind players like Juan Soto and Francisco Lindor. He said, “With Juan Soto getting on base as much as he does, alone, Bo Bichette would drive in 100 runs if he was healthy.”He continued, “With Francisco [Lindor] and Juan Soto getting on base as much as they do, we would be looking at [a lot of runs].”His point was simple. With two strong hitters getting on base often, Bichette will have more chances to bring them home.Last season with the Toronto Blue Jays, Bichette had 94 RBIs in 139 games. Injuries have slowed him down in recent years, but when he is fit, he is known for making steady contact and getting hits.Luino also explained the key difference between Bichette and Pete Alonso. He said, “We know Bichette, one of the big differences between him and Pete Alonso because this is relatively his spot, is that [Bichette] is going to put the ball in play a ton. He is going to get more hits.”He added that the Mets struggled last year when runners were on base but not scoring. “One of the biggest issues last year, not Pete Alonso’s fault, was driving in runners in scoring position … and having Soto and Lindor … it’s a match made in heaven.”While this debate continues, the Mets have also made another important move. Rookie Carson Benge has been named the starting right fielder for the 2026 season. He replaces Mike Tauchman, who is currently injured.Benge was the 19th overall pick in the 2024 MLB Draft. Last season, he moved quickly through the minor leagues, going from Single A to Triple A. He finished with a .281 batting average, .385 on base percentage, .472 slugging, along with 15 home runs and 73 RBIs. Now, with a new lineup and young talent stepping in, the Mets are clearly trying a different approach this season.
Article Summary: Marc Luino explains why Bo Bichette could fit better than Pete Alonso alongside Juan Soto and Francisco Lindor. The New York Mets are heading into the 2026 season with a very different team. Pete Alonso and Edwin Diaz are no longer part of the roster.

Article Title: Russell Wilson: Ciara’s ‘Girl Power’ gala look turns heads as Russell Wilson’s Amazon move follows close behind
Article Text:
Ciara’s red Con Cora look hits, but the bigger story is what the night signaled

Russell Wilson’s NFL future is still unclear, but his off-field playbook is loud





Ciara lit up her Instagram feed after a string of New York City appearances that blended fashion, philanthropy, and a very on brand business rollout from Russell Wilson On March 20, Ciara shared moments from the Con Cora Foundation event, where she wore a red sleeveless look with an ostrich-feather top and a thigh-high slit skirt, plus silver heels and a red bandana. She captioned the post, “Girl Power. Con Cora @KarolG 🫶🏽,” and included a shot with Karol G and Alicia Keys.The outfit did what it was supposed to do. It got people looking. But the bigger takeaway is how tightly the Wilson-Ciara brand machine is moving right now.The Con Cora gala raised funds to support the foundation’s mission of empowering young girls and women and backing their hopes and dreams. Ciara’s post leaned into that message, and the guest list did the rest.A few days later, the couple stepped out again, this time for the Fashion Scholarship Fund’s 89th annual gala on March 23. According to USA Today, they attended as honorees, and they brought their children onto the red carpet too. Ciara framed it as a deliberate choice, telling USA Today, “Clearly, they have a lot of energy, but it makes us so happy that they get to see us in moments like this because they are the future,” and, “And we just want them to be inspired in rooms like this and to see mommy and dad do what they do today at work.That gala centered on students pursuing fashion careers. The nonprofit awarded more than $1.9 million in scholarships to its 2026 class, per the same report.While Ciara has been the visual headline, Wilson is pushing the business side hard.On March 24, coverage noted that Wilson and Ciara launched “3Brand Jerseys,” described as a youth jersey brand sold exclusively on Amazon. In the announcement shared in the dump, Wilson said, “Welcome to @3BRAND Jerseys! Youth Sports means everything to our family and that’s why we’re launching our custom jerseys exclusively on Amazon. We are partnering to introduce Kit Your Team — fully customizable jerseys from @3BRAND. From youth leagues to rec squads to school teams, design your kit and bring your team identity to life. From Boys & Girls Flag Football to Soccer to Baseball & Basketball,” followed by, “Your colors.Your name. Your team. At your doorstep as soon as 48 hours. Welcome to the Family. Create your team kit now at the link in our bio,”That’s a lot of “what’s next” energy for a quarterback whose next team is still a question.Wilson’s on-field situation is messy on purpose right now. The dump notes his future remains unknown after finishing last season with the New York Giants. It also states he began the 2025 season as the Giants’ starter, then was moved out of the role as the team shifted toward rookie Jaxson Dart. During that stretch, Wilson said, “I want to be here… I still want to keep playing football.”The Fashion Scholarship Fund night also had a surprise moment from Travis Scott, who appeared to support scholarship recipients tied to his Cactus Jack Design Ethos program, saying, “Even today I was going through so much… there’s nothing that’s going to stop me from getting to see the future,” per the dump’s recap.
Article Summary: Ciara and Russell Wilson have been busy in New York City. The couple attended a Con Cora Foundation gala and a Fashion Scholarship Fund gala. Wilson also launched a youth jersey brand with Ciara. His NFL future is still unclear.

Article Title: Iga Swiatek: Iga Swiatek coaching rumors take twist as Piotr Wozniacki rejects role and backs Dawid Celt instead
Article Text:
Iga Swiatek (Image Via Getty)

Caroline Wozniacki’s father Piotr Wozniacki denies Iga Swiatek coaching role and suggests Dawid Celt





Iga Swiatek’s coaching situation has quickly become one of the biggest talking points in tennis. On March 23, 2026, the former World No. 1 confirmed that she had ended her partnership with Wim Fissette after a run of disappointing results. The news surprised many fans, especially because the pair had earlier found strong success together on the tour.Soon after the split, rumors started spreading that Piotr Wozniacki, father of former World No. 1 Caroline Wozniacki, could step in as Swiatek’s next coach. The talk picked up fast across tennis circles. But now, Piotr himself has come forward and made his position very clear, putting an end to all the speculation.Speaking to Interia Sport, Piotr Wozniacki directly addressed the rumors and left no room for confusion. He said, “I definitely won’t be Iga Świątek’s new coach. Someone passed this by. No one talked to me. And even if they did, I’d finished my coaching career.”He also shared why he does not want to return to coaching, even though he has received offers in the past. Piotr explained, “Honestly, after Karolina ended her career, I received very good offers, but now is not the right time.I have a wonderful family, grandchildren, granddaughters, and so on, and I know how much it costs. And I know how much energy, time, and true dedication it takes.”He added that his experience has taught him a lot, but he has already made his decision. “Of course, I also realize that people now want someone who is wiser about the mistakes they made, like I did with my daughter. And after all this experience, I know which direction to go, but that’s a done deal. Absolutely nothing can make me change my mind.”While ruling himself out, Piotr did offer a suggestion for Swiatek’s team. In the same interview with Interia Sport, he said, “If Iga Swiatek’s team, or Iga herself, hasn’t yet made a decision regarding Wim Fissette’s successor, I’m speaking out and I know what I’m talking about. Dawid Celt should be considered.”Dawid Celt is well known in Polish tennis. He worked as a hitting partner for Agnieszka Radwanska during her career and is currently the captain of Poland’s Billie Jean King Cup team. His name now adds another option as Swiatek looks ahead after a sudden coaching change.
Article Summary: Caroline Wozniacki’s father Piotr denies Iga Swiatek coaching role. He suggests Dawid Celt should be considered for the role. PiotR also revealed why he does not want to return to coaching.

Article Title: Jake Dennis: Jake Dennis had fiancee Lexi Grace Boosey by his side at Madrid E-Prix, couple shares their on and off-track moments
Article Text:
Jake Dennis' fiancee Lexi Grace Boosey has shared their candid moments from Madrid. (Image credit: Instagram)

Jake Dennis and Lexi Grace Boosey sum up Madrid trip

Jake Dennis on his contract extension with Andretti







Jake Dennis has opened up about his trip to Spain and how he managed to secure points after finishing sixth at the Madrid E-Prix. His fiancee, Lexi Grace Boosey, also accompanied him to Madrid and shared some candid glimpses from their visit. The couple have another connection with Spain, where they have been working on their new home in Marbella. Boosey has provided several updates on the interior work in recent weeks and hinted that they may be moving in soon.Jake Dennis recently signed a multi-year extension with Andretti, having been part of the team since his Formula E debut. He returned to the track following the announcement and finished sixth at the Circuito del Jarama.He took to Instagram to share a few pictures from the Round 6 race and wrote: “6th in Madrid. A messy weekend with a mix of all conditions, but we came away with some points. Not where we want to be as a team just yet, so some homework to do! Overall, what a great event it was for FE’s first time at Jarama. Awesome track and a fully sold-out event!”Sharing more pictures from off the track, his fiancee Lexi Grace Boosey captioned her post: “Madrid momento.”A few days earlier, she had shared several pictures of their new house in Spain and hinted at their move. “2.5 years down. 2 weeks to go,” she wrote in the caption.Jake Dennis began Season 12 with a win in Sao Paulo and later proposed to Boosey in December. He is currently eighth in the championship with 47 points.Speaking about his contract extension with Andretti during media day in Madrid, Dennis said, “Honestly, it was a pretty easy decision in the end. We had multiple options on the table, but from our side it was always Andretti. That was the priority, and we managed to come to an agreement on a multi-year deal, so everything is aligned with our future goals.”He added in an interview with Formulae.com, “It’s obvious we want to try and get back to the top. I’m excited to continue in Formula E as well. The GEN4 car sounds very promising and exciting, and the championship is moving to new levels with a longer calendar and more exciting locations. I think Formula E is in a great place right now. I’m happy to continue racing with Andretti.”He will return to action in Germany next month, with the Berlin E-Prix double-header scheduled from May 1-3.Also read: Andretti’s Jake Dennis and fiancee Lexi Grace Boosey share glimpses of their Marbella home under construction
Article Summary: Dennis finished sixth at the Madrid E-Prix. His fiancee, Lexi Grace Boosey, has shared some candid pictures from Madrid. The couple have also been working on their new home in Marbella. Dennis recently signed a multi-year extension with Andretti.

Article Title: Travis Kelce: ‘LETS GOOO’: Brittany Mahomes reacts as Travis Kelce’s $54.7 million Chiefs deal comes with a twist
Article Text:
Travis Kelce tells “Chiefs Kingdom” he is back for 2026 as Brittany Mahomes jumps into the comments with a two-word reaction. (Images via Getty)

Brittany Mahomes keeps it short while Travis Kelce tells Chiefs fans ‘it’s official’

Article Title: IPL 2026 captain's meet: When, where and the complete agenda
Article Text:
Captains of 10 IPL teams (Photo Credit: IPL)

What will be the agenda?

Watch RCB’s big changes ahead of IPL: New rules, tribute & squad update RCB’s big changes ahead of IPL: New rules, tribute & squad update

Ball Replacement Rules

in IPL Matches

Ball Change Rule in the Second Innings

Key Scenarios Explained

Scenario 1:

Scenario 2:

Scenario 3:





The captains of all IPL teams are set to meet with officials from the Board of Control for Cricket in India ( BCCI ) on March 25 to discuss a range of key issues, including the Impact Player rule and the code of conduct. However, the primary focus is expected to be on clarifications regarding the two-bouncer rule, bat checks, ball replacement, the use of saliva, and the retired-out provision. The IPL 2026 captains' meeting will take place at the BCCI headquarters in Mumbai.According to a BCCI communique to the franchises on Tuesday, accessed by Cricbuzz, Javagal Srinath and Nitin Menon - heads of the BCCI's match referees and umpires' panels, respectively - will address the captains.The meeting is scheduled from 4:30 pm to 6 pm on Wednesday (March 25).The BCCI has recently shared the playing conditions for the season with franchises, highlighting key changes to Rule 4.4 (change of ball in the second innings); 5.8.3 (bat checks); 18.5.1 and 18.5.2 (deliberate short runs); 19.5.2 (fielder grounded beyond the boundary); 28.7.6 (restriction on the placement of the fielder); and Appendix D-3.9 (combining umpire review with player review).A glance at the new season's playing conditions, in possession of this website, does not reveal any obvious changes, but the rules around ball replacement have been elaborated in detail - something that was absent in last year's playing conditions. Rule 4.4 in the latest playing conditions reads:If, during play, the ball is lost, cannot be recovered, or is deemed unfit for use due to normal wear and tear, the umpires will replace it with another ball that has a similar level of wear. Once the replacement is made, the umpires will inform both the batter and the fielding captain.In evening matches, the team bowling second is allowed to request one ball change after the completion of the 10th over. The request must be made only by the bowling captain and it must be made at the end of an over (not during an over). This rule applies only once in the second innings, regardless of dew conditions, replacement ball will have similar wear and tear as the previous one.Additionally, the umpires retain the authority to change the ball at any time during the match at their discretion.If the umpires replace the ball before the 10th over due to it being wet, damaged, lost, or out of shape, the fielding captain still retains the right to request a ball change after the 10th over. In this case, the umpires are required to approve the request.If the captain requests a ball change after the 10th over (e.g., in the 11th over) due to wet conditions and the umpires replace it: Any further request for a ball change later in the innings will be subject to the umpires’ discretion, not mandatory.If the captain requests a ball change during the 11th over due to damage or shape issues and the umpires agree: The team can still make their one mandatory request after the 10th over (e.g., due to dew later), and the umpires must approve it.There are also other items like team sheet, toss, broadcast media commitments and awards, about which there is no elaboration. The session will begin with an introduction by IPL COO Hemang Aming.
Article Summary: IPL 2026 captains' meeting will take place at the BCCI headquarters in Mumbai. The session will begin with an introduction by IPL COO Hemang Aming. The primary focus is expected to be on clarifications regarding the two-bouncer rule, bat checks, ball replacement, and the use of saliva.

Article Title: 'Your yorker is breathtaking': Mohammed Shami breathes fire, stumps shattered in nets - WATCH
Article Text:
Mohammed Shami (Image credit: LSG)

Watch RCB’s big changes ahead of IPL: New rules, tribute & squad update RCB’s big changes ahead of IPL: New rules, tribute & squad update





NEW DELHI: Lucknow Super Giants bowling coach Bharat Arun stood close to the stumps and threw the ball to the experienced Mohammed Shami . The veteran grabbed the ball, took his run-up, breathed fire, and did what he does best - destroyed the stumps with lethal yorkers.Shami is now 35, and his last appearance for India came during the 2025 Champions Trophy. But the hunger for a comeback was evident in his bowling. He hit the stumps repeatedly and rattled them with his yorkers during the nets.In fact, after the net session, Lucknow Super Giants head coach Justin Langer had a special mention for Shami in the team huddle."Your yorker... that yorker.. is breathtaking," Langer can be heard saying in a video posted by Lucknow Super Giants.Arun shaped India’s fast-bowling unit for a long time during his tenure as bowling coach. Under his stint, India achieved memorable Test wins, especially in overseas conditions, and Shami was one of the vital cogs in those victories."Shami and I share a special bond, and we've been together in the Indian team for seven years. I knew Shami even before that, and so that has only strengthened our bond. For me, working with Shami, I know what he brings to the table - he’s probably one of the best and better bowlers in world cricket today.It's very exciting for me to be working with Shami again," Arun told IANS.On paper, LSG’s pace attack looks strong with Shami, Anrich Nortje, Mayank Yadav, Mohsin Khan, Avesh Khan, Arjun Tendulkar, Naman Tiwari, Prince Yadav, and Akash Singh.Most of the bowlers are coming into the side after recovering from injuries.On being asked about managing workloads, Arun said: "I know that most of them are all coming from injuries, and that's why we had a pre-season camp about a month before we assembled in Lucknow, and that was to assess where they were at that point in time. Managing the fast-bowling workload is not only my job - it's also three of us together -- the physiotherapist, the strength and conditioning expert, and myself.""It's a combination of experience. If you look at Nortje and Shami, they bring in a lot of experience. Prince has done extremely well. Avesh and Mohsin are all exciting talents, while Naman, Akash, and Arjun are also there," he said."So if you look at the entire pace bowling setup, it's very, very healthy, and each one is unique and different, and thereby, how soon we get them to align with what the team wants and get the best out of them will be the biggest challenge," he added.LSG will open their IPL 2026 campaign against Delhi Capitals on April 1 at the Ekana Cricket Stadium in Lucknow.
Article Summary: Shami is now 35, and his last appearance for India came during the 2025 Champions Trophy. He hit the stumps repeatedly and rattled them with his yorkers during the nets. LSG will open their IPL 2026 campaign against Delhi Capitals on April 1.

Article Title: Facing legal charges, Yash Dayal won't be part of IPL
Article Text:
Yash Dayal (Image credit: BCCI/IPL)

Watch RCB’s big changes ahead of IPL: New rules, tribute & squad update RCB’s big changes ahead of IPL: New rules, tribute & squad update





BENGALURU : Putting speculation around Yash Dayal to rest, Royal Challengers Bengaluru’s director of cricket Mo Bobat on Tuesday confirmed that the leftarm pacer will not be part of this season’s IPL. Dayal, who has been dealing with legal issues, has not joined the RCB squad, which began training at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium last week. He has also been notably absent from the franchise’s social media posts, further fuelling uncertainty over his statusBobat said, “Yash will not be joining the squad as he is going through a personal situation. We’ve been very supportive of Yash to date and that’s reflected in the opportunity to either retain or release players.He remains under contract. We are communicating with him regularly but it has been decided that it is not in his or the franchise’s best interest for him to join up with us at this point.”Dayal, who has been facing criminal charges since July last year, missed the entire domestic season for UP.“Yash’s situation has been tricky. We went into our auction planning for a worst-case scenario, so we have recruited accordingly. We have some excellent domestic bowlers who are potential options,” added Bobat.RCB’s bowling concerns have been compounded by the continued absence of Australian quick Josh Hazlewood, who is still recovering from injury.“As soon as CA is happy, he’ll come over,” Bobat said.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru’s director of cricket Mo Bobat on Tuesday confirmed that the leftarm pacer will not be part of this season's IPL. Dayal, who has been dealing with legal issues, has not joined the RCB squad.

Article Title: Aditya Birla & TOI Groups, Bolt Ventures and Blackstone acquire RCB for $1.8bn
Article Text:
'We’ll build RCB into a global sporting institution'





MUMBAI: Aditya Birla Group, The Times of India Group, Bolt Ventures, and Blackstone have signed a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of Royal Challengers Bengaluru (RCB), current holders of both the Indian Premier League and Women's Premier League titles, from United Spirits Ltd (USL), a subsidiary of Diageo Plc. The transaction has valued the franchise at $1.78 billion (about Rs 16,600 crore).The acquisition is subject to customary closing conditions, including approval from BCCI, the IPL governing council, and other authorities.Under the new ownership structure, which will come into place after IPL 2026, Aryaman Vikram Birla, director at Aditya Birla Group, will serve as RCB chairman while Satyan Gajwani of The Times of India Group will be vice-chairman.Birla played for Madhya Pradesh and was also part of the Rajasthan Royals squad.In 2008, when BCCI floated IPL, United Spirits had submitted a winning bid of $111.6 million (approx Rs 485 crore) to acquire the franchise. In 2023, RCB paid Rs 901 crore to acquire the WPL team. In 2024, RCB won the WPL and followed it up with their first-ever IPL title win in 2025, becoming the first franchise to simultaneously hold both titles.The deal makes RCB the most expensive IPL franchise sold so far. Adar Poonawalla, Ranjan Pai of Manipal Hospitals, private equity firms EQT, TPG and Temasek, and the Glazer family, which owns Manchester United, were among other interested bidders.Commenting on the RCB deal, Aditya Birla Group chairman Kumar Mangalam Birla said, “Over the past two decades, IPL has morphed to become a global sporting powerhouse that has changed the face of Indian cricket, creating enormous value for India. RCB, as one of the most compelling franchises in modern sport, offers the Aditya Birla Group a distinctive platform to extend its legacy of institution-building into the arena of global sport. We are delighted to become custodians of this asset and committed to further building this extraordinary legacy.”Times Internet Ltd chairman Satyan Gajwani said, “RCB is the reigning champion and most popular brand in the IPL. As The Times of India Group, together with our partners, we will build RCB into a global sporting institution, while remaining rooted in Bengaluru and Karnataka and its incredible fanbase. We are committed to the people who built this championshipwinning culture — the players, coaches, the leadership team and the fans. We look forward to supporting the team as they take the pitch to defend RCB’s title.”David Blitzer, founder, Bolt Ventures, said, “RCB has a world-class fan base, and IPL is one of the great growth stories David Blitzer, founder, Bolt Ventures in global sport. Having invested in clubs and leagues around the world, I believe the opportunity at RCB stands out. We look forward to working alongside our partners and BCCI to build on the franchise’s championship success.”Viral Patel, CEO of BXPE, said, “We are excited to invest in RCB, building on Blackstone’s long-standing commitment to India. RCB stands out as one of the most popular sports franchises in the world with a powerful brand, a loyal fan base, and multiple avenues for growth. Together with our partners, we look forward to supporting the franchise’s enduring legacy and continued success.” The franchise has featured some of T20’s greatest icons including Virat Kohli, Chris Gayle, AB de Villiers, Shane Watson, Anil Kumble, Glenn Maxwell, Yuvraj Singh and Faf du Plessis.In 2013, after Kohli took over as captain, the franchise’s popularity catapulted. Kohli’s rise as Team India captain and cricket’s most authoritative brand coincided with RCB’s rise as one of IPL’s most followed teams. In 2025, Brand Finance had listed RCB as IPL’s second-most valuable brand after MI and among the three strongest IPL brands alongside Mumbai and Chennai. The same year, global investment bank Houlihan Lokey had also listed RCB as IPL’s foremost brand.
Article Summary: Aditya Birla Group, The Times of India Group, Bolt Ventures, and Blackstone have signed a definitive agreement to acquire 100% of Royal Challengers Bengaluru. The transaction has valued the franchise at $1.78 billion (about Rs 16,600 crore) The acquisition is subject to customary closing conditions, including approval from BCCI.

Article Title: Uk Asylum Case: 'Always been gay': Migrant granted asylum in Britain over homosexuality left wife and child in Africa
Article Text:
'Always been gay'





A migrant who was granted asylum in Britain after saying he was gay has admitted he left behind a wife and child in his homeland of Cameroon.When 35-year-old Marius Kamna first travelled to Britain from Cameroon on a temporary visa to attend a United Nations climate change conference in Glasgow, few would have expected him to ask for asylum. Instead of contributing to discussions on environmental issues, he applied for refugee protection, saying he was gay. He later settled in Wales and got a job of a heavy goods vehicle mechanic.Kamna’s claim of homosexuality was accepted by the British asylum panel, and he was granted refugee status.However, he did not mention that he had a wife and son in Cameroon when he applied for asylum. It later came out that he knew about them at the time but chose not to tell the authorities.Thousands of asylum seekers have based their claims on their sexual orientation, and some cases may involve people exploiting the system.In Kamna’s case, the revelation of a wife might fit this pattern. But Kamna still says that his situation is more complex.Speaking to the Daily Mail in Cardiff, where he works, he explained that he has “always been gay” and that his marriage in Cameroon was an attempt to escape homophobia in his home country.Cameroon is known for its harsh treatment of the LGBTQ+ community, with discrimination, stigma, and legal penalties still common.Kamna at first described himself as bisexual. He later corrected that statement: “No, that’s not true, I am gay. That is the way I’ve been since I was 15 or 16.” Kamna said he spent years hiding his true identity.Kamna said his marriage was arranged in Cameroon to win back the respect of his parents, who had disowned him when they discovered his sexuality. “I got chased out of the house by my parents and to earn back their respect a marriage was arranged,” he said.He said he asked for forgiveness and even paid an official a bribe to complete the paperwork. Although he is gay, Kamna admitted he went through with the marriage, which led to the birth of his son, Emanuel, who is now seven.Even though he has not seen his son since leaving Cameroon, Kamna said he stays in touch by phone and sends money to his child’s mother to support him. Kamna added that when he was invited to the Glasgow conference, he saw a chance to stay in Britain legally and start a new life, even if it meant leaving his family behind.Kamna said that arguments with neighbours in his shared house led to the police being called, and he has now moved to a new home in Newport. He added that his current partner is a gay man from Africa, and he is happy living in Britain.“When you come here you have to do everything right because you don’t want to go back,” he said.
Article Summary: Marius Kamna, 35, travelled to Britain on a temporary visa to attend a UN climate change conference in Glasgow. He applied for refugee protection, saying he was gay. Kamna’s claim of homosexuality was accepted by the British asylum panel, and he was granted refugee status. However, he did not mention that he had a wife and son in Cameroon when he applied for asylum.

Article Title: UK weather forecast: Snow to hit parts of Britain days after warmest day of year
Article Text:
‘Cold plunge’ expected by midweek

Snow possible in northern Scotland

Rain, wind to ease but chill to persist





Days after enjoying the warmest weather of the year so far, Britain is bracing for a sharp return to colder conditions, with temperatures expected to fall into single digits by midweek.Much of the UK saw pleasant sunshine and highs of around 20°C earlier this week, but forecasters say the mild spell has peaked. From Sunday onwards, temperatures will begin to drop steadily, marking a shift back to more typical early spring conditions.Meteorologists have warned of a noticeable chill setting in by Wednesday, when daytime temperatures for many areas will struggle to rise beyond single digits. Nights are expected to turn significantly colder, with the sharpest drop likely midweek.Conditions will also turn unsettled, with frequent showers and gusty winds affecting several parts of the country.Higher areas of northern Scotland could see snowfall from Wednesday night into Thursday morning. While not unusual for this time of year, the sudden change may feel stark after the recent stretch of warm and sunny weather.Forecasters noted that snow is most likely over hills, with lower regions mainly experiencing cold rain and strong winds.Wet and windy conditions are expected to affect northern areas early in the week, while other parts may see fog clearing to give way to bright spells. Rain will gradually move southeastwards before clearer but colder conditions set in.Later in the week, winds and rain are likely to ease, but temperatures are expected to remain on the cooler side, with a risk of overnight frost in some regions.Overall, the UK is heading into a markedly cooler phase, offering a sharp contrast to the unseasonably warm days seen just days earlier.
Article Summary: Britain is bracing for a sharp return to colder conditions, with temperatures expected to fall into single digits by midweek. Temperatures will begin to drop steadily from Sunday onwards, marking a shift back to more typical early spring conditions. Snow is most likely over hills, with lower regions mainly experiencing cold rain and strong winds. Rain will gradually move southeastwards before clearer but colder conditions set in.

Article Title: Why Hitler still finds admirers in Pakistan
Article Text: Admiration for Adolf Hitler should be morally unthinkable anywhere. Yet in parts of Pakistan, his name still surfaces in conversations with a strange tone of respect. The reaction can be startling for Europeans who encounter it. The German journalist Hasnain Kazim described precisely this experience in a widely discussed essay in Der Spiegel, where he wrote about repeatedly hearing Pakistanis speak of Hitler as if he were a bold or admirable historical figure.The encounters he describes are uncomfortable in their casualness. In one instance, a barber styling his hair remarks approvingly that he resembles Hitler. Taxi drivers and acquaintances bring up Hitler in conversation with Germans, assuming it will be taken as a compliment rather than an insult.The tone is not always ideological. Often it is simply admiration for what they imagine to be a powerful leader who stood up to Western powers.That perception rests on a deeply distorted understanding of history. In many of these conversations Hitler appears not as the architect of genocide, but as a figure associated with strength, discipline and defiance. The Holocaust is rarely mentioned. The scale of Nazi crimes is either unknown or ignored.Instead, Hitler becomes a vague symbol of power.The essay recounts one particularly surreal image from Islamabad: a luxury car carrying a swastika sticker alongside the words “I like Nazi.” The symbolism appears almost casual, stripped of the horror that it carries in Europe. What shocks Germans encountering such moments is not simply the presence of Nazi imagery but the absence of shame surrounding it.The roots of this phenomenon are complicated but not entirely mysterious. Anti-Western sentiment has long shaped political narratives in Pakistan, particularly those rooted in resentment toward former colonial powers and contemporary global politics. In that emotional landscape, figures who are perceived to have challenged Western dominance sometimes acquire an undeserved aura of admiration. Hitler is occasionally inserted into that narrative as a supposed opponent of Britain and the West, even though the historical reality is far more complex and morally catastrophic.Another factor is the persistence of conspiratorial thinking and antisemitic tropes. In some discussions Hitler appears as a figure connected to hostility toward Jews and Israel, particularly in conversations shaped by anger over the Palestinian issue. In such contexts the Holocaust fades from view and the dictator becomes a symbol in a political story that has little to do with the reality of Nazi Germany.This distorted memory is not unique to Pakistan. Around the world, historical figures sometimes become detached from the events that defined them. Distance, ignorance and grievance can transform villains into caricatures. What remains is a simplified image of power without the moral weight that should accompany it.The disturbing element lies in the casualness. Hitler is sometimes invoked as a rhetorical flourish or a symbol of strong leadership rather than as the man responsible for one of the worst crimes in human history. When that happens, the historical context collapses. The genocide, the ideology and the devastation of the Second World War vanish from the conversation.The essay captures this unsettling contrast. For Germans, Hitler represents an enduring national trauma and a warning about the dangers of extremism. In some parts of Pakistan, however, the same name appears stripped of its historical meaning and recast as a figure of exaggerated strength.That gap in historical memory illustrates how easily symbols can travel across cultures while losing the reality that gave them meaning. Hitler becomes less a historical figure and more a mythological one, shaped by hearsay, resentment and incomplete education.The tragedy is that admiration of this kind often grows not from ideological commitment but from ignorance. When history is poorly understood, the past becomes a collection of symbols rather than lessons. In that environment even the darkest figures can be misunderstood as heroes.The uncomfortable truth revealed in the essay is therefore not simply that Hitler has admirers in Pakistan. It is that admiration can exist without a clear understanding of what Hitler actually did. When historical knowledge fades, even the most obvious villains can be recast in the imagination as symbols of strength.And that, perhaps, is the most disturbing lesson of all.
Article Summary: Admiration for Adolf Hitler should be morally unthinkable anywhere. Yet in parts of Pakistan, his name still surfaces in conversations with a strange tone of respect. The tone is not always ideological. Often it is simply admiration for what they imagine to be a powerful leader.

Article Title: China Dog Escape: Seven stolen dogs escape moving truck in China, travel 17 km together to return to their owners
Article Text:
Seven dogs break free from thieves and travel 17 km together to return to their village in China.







The video of seven dogs from different breeds traveling together, uncertain about where to go, stopping to see curious passersby, has been doing the rounds. As social media users ask Grok whether the video is real, the South China Morning Post reported that the seven dogs walking along a busy highway in Changchun, Jilin province, were spotted by a netizen surnamed Lu.They were stolen by thieves working for a dog meat shop and were whisked away in a truck. But the seven dogs escaped from the truck and returned home, traveling 17 km in two days.The group of dogs included a German Shepherd, which was injured, a Corgi, Golden Retreivers, Labradors and Pekinese dogs.“They resemble a band of little brothers in distress, moving in unison – nothing like stray dogs," Lu told Dahe Daily. Lu said he tried to help the dogs several times, wanted to guide them to safety, but the dogs kept on walking and ignored his attempts.Lu shared the video on Douyin, urging the authorities to step in.The dogs were seen wandering through nearby fields by some others, too, along the way. As their videos started going viral on Chinese social media, volunteers tracked their movement and ensured that they were home safely. Bitter Coffee Stray Dog Base told SCMP that the dogs are from the save village and they know each other as they often play together and that's why they remained in a pack for two days as they returned home.One volunteer claimed that individuals operating a dog meat shop had stolen the dogs, suggesting they may have escaped from a truck, though no witnesses saw their departure.Based on the timeline of the videos of them walking back home, it's believed that they escaped on March 16 and returned to their respective homes on March 18.In China, dog theft is considered a criminal offence, with penalties that can include fines or imprisonment based on the value of the animal.
Article Summary: Seven dogs from different breeds travel 17 km together to return to their village in China. They were stolen by thieves working for a dog meat shop and were whisked away in a truck. Dog theft is considered a criminal offence in China, with penalties that can include fines or imprisonment.
